serving.natural_language_portion: One large cup of King Jasmine Milk Tea typically contains 500 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from similar milk tea entries in HPB Foodo, as exact 'King Jasmine Milk Tea' was not found. Values from input JSON were used as a base and validated against HPB for plausibility.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values for Common Foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI estimated based on sweetened milk tea and similar sugar-sweetened beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database. The high sugar content (8g/100g) and lack of fiber contribute to a medium GI.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (8g/100g) in a beverage with no fiber.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this sugar-sweetened beverage, opting for unsweetened alternatives to mitigate sugar-driven blood pressure risks.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage (8g sugar/100g) with 0g fiber, leading to rapid glucose absorption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sugar content and lack of fiber, which can cause significant postprandial glucose spikes; unsweetened options are strongly recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar content (8g/100g) and saturated fat (1.2g/100g).
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular health, it is advisable to consume this milk tea in moderation or choose unsweetened versions, as excessive intake of added sugars and saturated fats can negatively impact heart health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar content (8g/100g) which can promote inflammatory responses.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption of this sweetened milk tea, as its added sugar content may exacerbate systemic inflammation; unsweetened jasmine green tea is a better choice.

llm_training.natural_language_description: King Jasmine Milk Tea is a refreshing beverage originating from Singapore, characterized by its blend of 9-scented jasmine tea, dairy milk, and real cane sugar, typically served chilled. It offers a sweet and floral taste profile with a creamy texture. Nutritionally, it is a source of carbohydrates and sugars, with moderate fat and protein content, and negligible fiber. Due to its high sugar content, it is generally not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. It contains dairy, making it unsuitable for those with lactose intolerance or dairy allergies, but is vegetarian and gluten-free. The drink is a popular choice in Southeast Asian beverage culture, often enjoyed as a treat or refreshment.
